comment_text.sn3.14:1.2: This was obviously after Ajātasattu’s regicide of his father Bimbisāra, and must therefore be in the last years of the Buddha’s life. It is just one of the indications of Ajātasattu’s aggressive nature and the breakdown of peace in the Buddha’s final years. | Kāsi, of which Varanasi was the capital, had lost its status as an independent kingdom to Pasenadi’s father. Here it has become the proxy for the struggle for dominance between the two largest kingdoms of the time, Kosala and Magadha, a struggle that Magadha would ultimately win. | The four divisions of the army are defined in the next Sutta (SN 3.15:1.9).
comment_text.sn3.14:4.1: One of his bad friends was Devadatta.
comment_text.sn3.14:4.2: The Buddha taught Pasenadi the value of good friends at SN 3.18.

root_text.sn3.14:1.2: Atha kho rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ abbhuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.14:1.4: “rājā kira māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā mamaṁ abbhuyyāto yena kāsī”ti.
root_text.sn3.14:1.5: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ paccuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.14:1.6: Atha kho rājā ca māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājā ca pasenadi kosalo saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.sn3.14:1.7: Tasmiṁ kho pana saṅgāme rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ parājesi.
root_text.sn3.14:1.8: Parājito ca rājā pasenadi kosalo sakameva rājadhāniṁ sāvatthiṁ paccuyyāsi.
root_text.sn3.14:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
root_text.sn3.14:2.2: Sāvatthiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn3.14:3.1: “Idha, bhante, rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ abbhuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.14:3.2: Assosi kho, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo:
root_text.sn3.14:3.3: ‘rājā kira māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā mamaṁ abbhuyyāto yena kāsī’ti.
root_text.sn3.14:3.4: Atha kho, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo caturaṅginiṁ senaṁ sannayhitvā rājānaṁ māgadhaṁ ajātasattuṁ vedehiputtaṁ paccuyyāsi yena kāsi.
root_text.sn3.14:3.5: Atha kho, bhante, rājā ca māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājā ca pasenadi kosalo saṅgāmesuṁ.
root_text.sn3.14:3.6: Tasmiṁ kho pana, bhante, saṅgāme rājā māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ parājesi.
root_text.sn3.14:3.7: Parājito ca, bhante, rājā pasenadi kosalo sakameva rājadhāniṁ sāvatthiṁ paccuyyāsī”ti.
root_text.sn3.14:4.1: “Rājā, bhikkhave, māgadho ajātasattu vedehiputto pāpamitto pāpasahāyo pāpasampavaṅko;
root_text.sn3.14:4.2: rājā ca kho, bhikkhave, pasenadi kosalo kalyāṇamitto kalyāṇasahāyo kalyāṇasampavaṅko.
root_text.sn3.14:4.3: Ajjeva, bhikkhave, rājā pasenadi kosalo imaṁ rattiṁ dukkhaṁ seti parājito”ti.

translation_text.sn3.14:1.2: Then King Ajātasattu of Magadha, son of the princess of Videha, mobilized an army of four divisions and marched to Kāsi to attack King Pasenadi of Kosala.
translation_text.sn3.14:1.3: When King Pasenadi heard of this,
translation_text.sn3.14:1.5: he mobilized an army of four divisions and marched to Kāsi to defend it against Ajātasattu.
translation_text.sn3.14:1.6: Then the two kings met in battle.
translation_text.sn3.14:1.7: And in that battle Ajātasattu defeated Pasenadi,
translation_text.sn3.14:1.8: who withdrew to his own capital at Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn3.14:2.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.sn3.14:2.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened. Then the Buddha said:
translation_text.sn3.14:4.1: “Mendicants, King Ajātasattu has bad friends, companions, and associates.
translation_text.sn3.14:4.2: But King Pasenadi has good friends, companions, and associates.
translation_text.sn3.14:4.3: Yet on this day King Pasenadi will have a bad night’s sleep as one defeated.”
translation_text.sn3.14:4.4: That is what the Buddha said. …
translation_text.sn3.14:5.4: having left victory and defeat behind.”

variant_text.sn3.14:1.8: sakameva → saṅgāmā (mr) | paccuyyāsi → pāyāsi (bj, pts1ed); paccāyāsi (pts2ed)

reference_text.sn3.14:1.1: bj13.154, cck15.114, ms12S1_658, msdiv125, ndp12.82, pts-vp-pli1ed1.83, pts-vp-pli2ed1.188
